title: boraginaceae
intro: The boraginaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. This family consists of approximately 2,000 species of plants, including borage, forget-me-not, and heliotrope, which are found in a variety of habitats worldwide. The plants within this family are often characterized by their distinctive blue or purple flowers, and are commonly used in gardens and landscapes for their ornamental value. Many species in the boraginaceae family also have edible leaves and flowers, making them a popular choice for herb gardens and culinary use.
